history: The client has a history of recurring conflicts and strained relationships, often finding solace in the structured world of mathematics. They have ongoing trust and boundary issues in relationships, some of which involve alcoholism. The client has also exhibited aggressive behavior in the past.
intermediate_belief: If I focus on mathematics and intellectual pursuits, then I can avoid the pain of loneliness and the complexities of relationships.
intermediate_belief_depression: If I can't find solace in mathematics, then I will be overwhelmed by loneliness and sadness.
coping_strategies: The client immerses themselves in intellectual pursuits, particularly mathematics, to avoid confronting loneliness. They find refuge in the clarity and logic of math, which contrasts with the unpredictability of personal relationships.
situation: Spending holidays alone and reflecting on feelings of sadness and loneliness.
auto_thought: I am alone and will always be alone.
behavior: The client immerses themselves in mathematical work, focusing on teaching and understanding complex concepts to distract from feelings of loneliness.
